expectations.evidence: File saved as '03-prompt-template-engine-walkthrough.md' — uses 'walkthrough' suffix instead of the skill's '--pseudocode.md' naming convention.

expectations.text: Has a Walkthrough section split into named Units (not a flat checklist)
expectations.evidence: Six named UNIT sections (UNIT 1 through UNIT 6) starting at L102.

expectations.text: Every Unit has Purpose, Inputs/outputs, Pseudocode, and a Touch list
expectations.evidence: Units have 'Goal' and Pseudocode but no explicit 'Inputs / outputs:' subsection and no 'Touch list' subsection (paths are inlined in prose and a top-level 'Repo conventions to mimic' section instead).

expectations.text: Touch list paths exist in the ACE repo at run time
expectations.evidence: All inlined paths (modelResolver.js, promptBuilder.js, dbStatus.js, AIModel.test.js, graph/prompts.js) are real.

expectations.text: Units reference models/PromptTemplate.js, lib/ai/promptResolver.js, config/prompts-default.json, scripts/seedPrompts.js, graph/prompts.js
expectations.evidence: All five files appear across the six Units.

expectations.text: Includes End-to-end sequence section tying Units together
expectations.evidence: Has 'Build order' section (L87) which is functionally similar but not labeled 'End-to-end sequence' as the template requires; no runtime-failure-mode walkthrough.

expectations.text: Pseudocode describes shape (LRU cache, resolver, fallback) without becoming a paste-ready implementation
expectations.evidence: UNIT 3 pseudocode is more elaborated than the skill version but still pseudocode-shaped with decision callouts.
Section 4 'Verification steps' at L505 lists the ticket jest commands and seed runs.
